basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: al-Ṭūr — “The Mount” — opens with a fivefold oath: Mount Sinai, the inscribed Book, the frequented House, the lofty vault, the brimming sea. Qushayrī makes of the Ṭūr the place where the beloved's foot alighted at the hour of the Discourse, and of the frequented House the hearts of the gnostics, peopled with His love. The oath answers: the punishment of your Lord is inevitable — meaning, outwardly, the Fire, and inwardly the veil after presence, occultation after unveiling. The sūra opposes the torment of the deniers (thrust into the Fire, to whom patience no longer serves) to the felicity of the godfearing: gardens, houris, the reunion of lineages, and above all the absence in God — the blessed are ravished far from whoever serves them: the drink enchants them, but not by the one who pours it, for the intimacy there is with the Real. It defends the Prophet against the accusations (soothsayer, madman, poet), unfolds the great series of “am” that dismantles every pretension of the deniers, and is sealed on patience under the divine gaze: you are before Our eyes, and the continuous glorification that makes patience light.
themes: the oath by the Mount and the Book

versets.ar: وَالطُّورِ
versets.tr: wa-ṭ-ṭūri
versets.trad: By the Mount!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Ṭūr is the mountain on which God spoke to Moses, for it was the place where the beloved's foot alighted at the hour of hearing the Discourse; and it was there that Moses heard the mention of Muḥammad and his community, until God called us while we were still in the loins of our fathers: “I have given you before you asked Me.” The oath opens a sūra where creation attests the inevitable punishment.

mots.racine: ط و ر
mots.sens: by the Mount
mots.def: al-Ṭūr: Mount Sinai, the place of the Discourse to Moses.

versets.ar: وَكِتَابٍ مَّسْطُورٍ
versets.tr: wa-kitābin masṭūrin
versets.trad: by a Book inscribed
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: a Book inscribed in the scrolls and in the preserved Tablet; it is said: the book of the angels in heaven, where they read what was and what will be; it is said: what God has written upon Himself of mercy toward His servants; or His word: “My mercy has outstripped My wrath.” The oath by the heavenly Scripture announces the truth of the recorded decree.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / س ط ر
mots.sens: by an inscribed Book

versets.ar: فِي رَقٍّ مَّنشُورٍ
versets.tr: fī raqqin manshūrin
versets.trad: on a parchment unrolled!
versets.commentaire: The Book is “on an unrolled parchment” (raqq manshūr). Qushayrī refers this parchment back to the Book already mentioned — the preserved Tablet or the scrolls of deeds. The raqq unrolled, without seal or fold, speaks the evidence of what is inscribed: the divine decree is not hidden but spread out for whoever can read.

mots.racine: ر ق ق / ن ش ر
mots.sens: on an unrolled parchment

versets.ar: وَالْبَيْتِ الْمَعْمُورِ
versets.tr: wa-l-bayti l-maʿmūri
versets.trad: by the Frequented House!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: al-Bayt al-Maʿmūr is in the fourth heaven; it is said: it is the hearts of the worshipping gnostics, peopled with His love and His knowledge; it is said: the places of their worship and the assemblies of their retreats; it is said: the Kaʿba. The “frequented” House (maʿmūr) has its inner counterpart: the heart inhabited by the remembrance of God is itself a sanctuary visited by the angels.

mots.racine: ب ي ت / ع م ر
mots.sens: by the frequented House
mots.def: al-Bayt al-Maʿmūr: the much-visited heavenly House.

versets.ar: وَالسَّقْفِ الْمَرْفُوعِ
versets.tr: wa-s-saqfi l-marfūʿi
versets.trad: by the lofty vault!
versets.commentaire: “By the raised roof” (al-saqf al-marfūʿ) — the sky. Qushayrī adds: it is said: the sky of their aspirations in the kingdom (al-malakūt). Above the visible roof of the heavens is the inner firmament of high aspirations: the gnostic's heart, too, has its lofty vault toward the world of realities.

mots.racine: س ق ف / ر ف ع
mots.sens: by the lofty vault

versets.ar: وَالْبَحْرِ الْمَسْجُورِ
versets.tr: wa-l-baḥri l-masjūri
versets.trad: by the swelling sea!
versets.commentaire: “By the swollen sea” (al-baḥr al-masjūr) — the brimming seas. The fivefold oath — the Mount, the Book, the House, the vault, the sea — embraces the heights and the depths, the revealed and the hidden. All creation is summoned to attest the inevitable punishment that the next verse affirms.

mots.racine: ب ح ر / س ج ر
mots.sens: by the brimming sea

versets.ar: إِنَّ عَذَابَ رَبِّكَ لَوَاقِعٌ
versets.tr: inna ʿadhāba rabbika la-wāqiʿun
versets.trad: The punishment of your Lord is inevitable;
versets.commentaire: The answer to the oath: the punishment of your Lord will come about (wāqiʿ). Qushayrī gives the punishment an outer and inner sense: outwardly, that with which He threatened His rebellious servants; inwardly, the veil after presence, occultation after unveiling, rejection after acceptance. The worst penalty, for the lover, is not the fire but the distancing after nearness.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ر ب ب / و ق ع
mots.sens: the punishment of your Lord is inevitable
mots.def: ʿadhāb: punishment; wāqiʿ: that will come about.

versets.ar: مَّا لَهُ مِن دَافِعٍ
versets.tr: mā lahu min dāfiʿin
versets.trad: nothing can repel it,
versets.commentaire: “None can repel it” (dāfiʿ). Qushayrī: when He repels a servant, the decree of His rejection is sealed. And he cites: “if my soul has turned from a thing, never again, by any means, will it return to it.” The punishment of rejection, once pronounced, is without appeal: none can avert what God has decided.

mots.racine: د ف ع
mots.sens: it has no repeller

versets.ar: يَوْمَ تَمُورُ السَّمَاءُ مَوْرًا
versets.tr: yawma tamūru s-samāʾu mawran
versets.trad: the Day the sky sways in turmoil
versets.commentaire: “The Day the sky whirls in turmoil” (tamūr mawran). Qushayrī: it turns with what it contains. The sky, image of stability, is seized with vertigo: the great upheaval begins with the vault the oath has just invoked (verse 5). What seemed fixed is set in motion on the announced Day.

mots.racine: م و ر
mots.sens: the day the sky whirls
mots.gram: complement of time + verb + cognate accusative

versets.ar: وَتَسِيرُ الْجِبَالُ سَيْرًا
versets.tr: wa-tasīru l-jibālu sayran
versets.trad: and the mountains set into motion,
versets.commentaire: “And the mountains set into motion” (tasīru sayran), leaving their places. Qushayrī: they will move from their sites. What was most rooted in the earth sets in motion: on the Day of punishment, no foothold remains. The cosmos itself loses its bearings.

mots.racine: س ي ر / ج ب ل
mots.sens: the mountains will move

versets.ar: فَوَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ
versets.tr: fa-waylun yawmaʾidhin lil-mukadhdhibīna
versets.trad: Woe, that Day, to the deniers,
versets.commentaire: “Woe, that Day, to the deniers” (mukadhdhibīn). Qushayrī: wayl is a word the Arabs say to whoever falls into perdition. Woe strikes those who denied the truth: at the moment the sky whirls and the mountains march, their denial turns against them.

mots.racine: و ي ل / ك ذ ب
mots.sens: woe that day to the deniers

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ هُمْ فِي خَوْضٍ يَلْعَبُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna hum fī khawḍin yalʿabūna
versets.trad: who toy with grave matters in jest.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: in the falsehood of denial they plunge and play. The khawḍ is immersion in vain talk; they treat in jest what is gravest. Frivolity before the truth is no innocence but blindness: one plays with the sacred only because one has already lost the sense of seriousness.

mots.racine: خ و ض / ل ع ب
mots.sens: in falsehood they play
mots.def: khawḍ: immersion in the vain; laʿiba: to play.

versets.ar: يَوْمَ يُدَعُّونَ إِلَىٰ نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ دَعًّا
versets.tr: yawma yudaʿʿūna ilā nāri jahannama daʿʿan
versets.trad: On the Day they are thrust into the Fire of Hell:
versets.commentaire: “The Day they are thrust toward the Fire with a thrust” (daʿʿan). Qushayrī: they are cast toward the Fire with a brutal blow. The verb daʿʿ denotes the violent shoving: they who took everything in jest are now driven by force. Contempt for the truth reverses into endured constraint.

mots.ar: يَوْمَ يُدَعُّونَ إِلَىٰ نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ دَعًّا
mots.tr: yawma yudaʿʿūna ilā nāri jahannama daʿʿan
mots.racine: د ع ع / ن و ر / ج ه ن م
mots.sens: the day they are thrust toward the Fire
mots.gram: complement of time + passive verb + cognate accusative

versets.ar: هَٰذِهِ النَّارُ الَّتِي كُنتُم بِهَا تُكَذِّبُونَ
versets.tr: hādhihi n-nāru llatī kuntum bihā tukadhdhibūna
versets.trad: “This is the Fire which you used to deny!
versets.commentaire: “This is the Fire you used to deny!” It is said to them face to face. What they denied in jest (verse 12) rises before them as an inescapable reality. Denial does not abolish the Fire; it only delays the confrontation, all the more crushing as it gives the lie to their mockery.

mots.ar: هَٰذِهِ النَّارُ الَّتِي كُنتُم بِهَا تُكَذِّبُونَ
mots.tr: hādhihi n-nāru llatī kuntum bihā tukadhdhibūn
mots.racine: ن و ر / ك ذ ب
mots.sens: this is the Fire you denied

versets.ar: أَفَسِحْرٌ هَٰذَا أَمْ أَنتُمْ لَا تُبْصِرُونَ
versets.tr: a-fa-siḥrun hādhā am antum lā tubṣirūna
versets.trad: Is this magic, or do you not see?
versets.commentaire: They are asked with irony: “Is this magic, or did you not see?” Qushayrī: is it of the kind of magic, as you claimed, or were your sights veiled? What they called “magic” about the Prophet they find real: either they were lying, or they were blind. Both condemn them.

mots.racine: س ح ر
mots.sens: is this magic?

mots.racine: ب ص ر
mots.sens: or do you not see?

versets.ar: اصْلَوْهَا فَاصْبِرُوا أَوْ لَا تَصْبِرُوا سَوَاءٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ۖ إِنَّمَا تُجْزَوْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: iṣlawhā fa-ṣbirū aw lā taṣbirū sawāʾun ʿalaykum innamā tujzawna mā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: Burn in it! Bear it or do not bear it — it is all the same for you. You are only recompensed for what you did!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: patience before the final recompense has no value, for their punishment is a sanction. Here patience (ṣabr) no longer serves: to bear it or not, the result is the same. On earth, patience transforms the trial; in the punishment, it no longer eases it. “You are only recompensed for your deeds”: the torment is the pure mirror of the work.

mots.racine: ص ل ي / ص ب ر
mots.sens: burn in it, bear it or not
mots.def: ṣaliya: to burn; ṣabara: to bear.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / ع م ل
mots.sens: you are recompensed only for your deeds

versets.ar: إِنَّ الْمُتَّقِينَ فِي جَنَّاتٍ وَنَعِيمٍ
versets.tr: inna l-muttaqīna fī jannātin wa-naʿīmin
versets.trad: Truly, the godfearing will be in gardens and delight,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the godfearing are in gardens and delight, in the present as in the future — and he specifies that the anticipated delight is union and nearness. The Sufis live their journey as an intermediate life with its resurrections: they taste here below a foretaste of Paradise, through the states of presence and intimacy.

mots.racine: و ق ي / ج ن ن / ن ع م
mots.sens: the godfearing are in gardens and delight

versets.ar: فَاكِهِينَ بِمَا آتَاهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ وَوَقَاهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ عَذَابَ الْجَحِيمِ
versets.tr: fākihīna bimā ātāhum rabbuhum wa-waqāhum rabbuhum ʿadhāba l-jaḥīmi
versets.trad: rejoicing in what their Lord has given them. And their Lord has spared them the punishment of the Blaze.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “fākihīn” means rejoicing (muʿjabīn) at what their Lord has given them; it is also said: supplied with fruit (fākiha), as one says of a man “tāmir” (who has dates). A twofold grace: the enjoyment of the gift and preservation from the Fire. To be spared the punishment is itself a delight, sometimes more precious than the positive gift.

mots.racine: ف ك ه / أ ت ي
mots.sens: rejoicing in what their Lord gave them

mots.racine: و ق ي / ع ذ ب / ج ح م
mots.sens: their Lord spared them the punishment of the Blaze

versets.ar: كُلُوا وَاشْرَبُوا هَنِيئًا بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: kulū wa-shrabū hanīʾan bimā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: “Eat and drink in peace, for what you did,
versets.commentaire: “Eat and drink in peace” (hanīʾan). Qushayrī distinguishes the degrees of delight: for some, it is sweet by taste and savour; for others, the hearing of His word is sweet to them; for others still, it is sweet and light while they are in His presence. The same feast is savoured differently by the depth of the heart: from the pleasure of the senses to the sweetness of the divine hearing.

mots.racine: أ ك ل / ش ر ب / ه ن أ
mots.sens: eat and drink in peace

mots.racine: ع م ل
mots.sens: for what you did

versets.ar: مُتَّكِئِينَ عَلَىٰ سُرُرٍ مَّصْفُوفَةٍ ۖ وَزَوَّجْنَاهُم بِحُورٍ عِينٍ
versets.tr: muttakiʾīna ʿalā sururin maṣfūfatin wa-zawwajnāhum bi-ḥūrin ʿīnin
versets.trad: reclining on couches in rows.” And We will wed them to wide-eyed houris.
versets.commentaire: Reclining on couches in rows (maṣfūfa), wedded to the wide-eyed houris. Qushayrī: they remain in joy and gladness, and an abundant share of intimacy (uns). The repose of Paradise is not only physical: it is fullness of intimacy, where the fulfilled being is delivered from all lack and all tension.

mots.racine: و ك أ / س ر ر / ص ف ف
mots.sens: reclining on couches in rows
mots.def: surur: couches; maṣfūfa: arrayed in rows.

mots.racine: ز و ج / ح و ر / ع ي ن
mots.sens: We wedded them to wide-eyed houris

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَاتَّبَعَتْهُمْ ذُرِّيَّتُهُم بِإِيمَانٍ أَلْحَقْنَا بِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ وَمَا أَلَتْنَاهُم مِّنْ عَمَلِهِم مِّن شَيْءٍ ۚ كُلُّ امْرِئٍ بِمَا كَسَبَ رَهِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-ttabaʿathum dhurriyyatuhum bi-īmānin alḥaqnā bihim dhurriyyatahum wa-mā alatnāhum min ʿamalihim min shayʾin kullu mriʾin bimā kasaba rahīnun
versets.trad: Those who believed and whose offspring followed them in faith — We will join their offspring to them, and We will not deprive them of anything of their deeds. Every man is held in pledge for what he has earned.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their joy is made complete by the reunion of their offspring to them, for to enjoy a blessing alone, apart from those the heart loves — family, children, posterity — spoils the felicity. And he cites Joseph: “bring me all your family.” God reunites the lineages without diminishing anyone's deeds: “every man is the pledge (rahīn) of what he has earned” — the grace of reunion does not exempt from personal responsibility.

mots.racine: ل ح ق / ذ ر ر
mots.sens: We joined their offspring to them

mots.racine: ك س ب / ر ه ن
mots.sens: every man is pledge for what he earned
mots.def: rahīn: pledge, hostage; kasaba: to earn.

versets.ar: وَأَمْدَدْنَاهُم بِفَاكِهَةٍ وَلَحْمٍ مِّمَّا يَشْتَهُونَ
versets.tr: wa-amdadnāhum bi-fākihatin wa-laḥmin mimmā yashtahūna
versets.trad: We will supply them with fruit and meat of their desire.
versets.commentaire: God supplies fruit and meat according to their appetite (yashtahūn). In Paradise, desire itself, which on earth is a source of lack, becomes pure fulfilled gladness. What was anxious craving turns into a continuous gift, adjusted to the wish: felicity answers the heart even before it voices its demand.

mots.racine: م د د / ف ك ه / ل ح م
mots.sens: We supplied them with fruit and meat

mots.racine: ش ه و
mots.sens: of what they desire

versets.ar: يَتَنَازَعُونَ فِيهَا كَأْسًا لَّا لَغْوٌ فِيهَا وَلَا تَأْثِيمٌ
versets.tr: yatanāzaʿūna fīhā kaʾsan lā laghwun fīhā wa-lā taʾthīmun
versets.trad: They will pass around a cup wherein is neither idle talk nor sin.
versets.commentaire: They pass a cup “with no vain talk nor sin.” Qushayrī: no falsehood is said among them, no sin reaches them, as happens among drinkers here below; and the wine does not lead their reason astray to the point of making them lose the bounds of adab. And he adds: how should their assembly be otherwise, when they know who gives them to drink, and they are in His presence and under His gaze?

mots.racine: ن ز ع / ك أ س
mots.sens: they pass a cup
mots.def: tanāzaʿa: to pass mutually; kaʾs: cup.

mots.racine: ل غ و / أ ث م
mots.sens: no idle talk nor sin
mots.def: laghw: vain talk; taʾthīm: what causes sin.

versets.ar: وَيَطُوفُ عَلَيْهِمْ غِلْمَانٌ لَّهُمْ كَأَنَّهُمْ لُؤْلُؤٌ مَّكْنُونٌ
versets.tr: wa-yaṭūfu ʿalayhim ghilmānun lahum ka-annahum luʾluʾun maknūnun
versets.trad: Youths will circulate among them, like hidden pearls.
versets.commentaire: Young servants circulate, like the preserved pearl. Qushayrī unfolds here a major pearl: the blessed are ravished far from the abode and from whoever is in it, by the grip of what absorbs them: the drink enchants them — but not by the one who serves it. If already here below the servant, were he in prison all his life, knows an hour in which he abstracts himself from the hearing of creatures and the sight of a single being, even a beloved son or a tender brother — how could one think that in Paradise he would be brought back from the highest to the lowest? The intimacy there is with the Real, not the created.

mots.racine: ط و ف / غ ل م
mots.sens: youths circulate among them

mots.racine: ل أ ل أ / ك ن ن
mots.sens: like preserved pearls

versets.ar: وَأَقْبَلَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ يَتَسَاءَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-aqbala baʿḍuhum ʿalā baʿḍin yatasāʾalūna
versets.trad: They will turn to one another, questioning.
versets.commentaire: The blessed turn to one another and question. After the absence in God (verse 24), here is the peaceful exchange: they recall together their past. The concord of Paradise is no mute solitude but serene communion, where one shares the memory of grace received.

mots.racine: ق ب ل
mots.sens: they turned to one another

mots.racine: س أ ل
mots.sens: they question one another

versets.ar: قَالُوا إِنَّا كُنَّا قَبْلُ فِي أَهْلِنَا مُشْفِقِينَ
versets.tr: qālū innā kunnā qablu fī ahlinā mushfiqīna
versets.trad: They will say: “Formerly, among our people, we were apprehensive.
versets.commentaire: “We were formerly, among our own, full of fear (mushfiqīn).” Qushayrī: they recall the apprehension that dwelt in them about their fate. The reverential fear (ishfāq) that held them on earth is revealed, in Paradise, as the seed of their present security: whoever feared was preserved.

mots.ar: إِنَّا كُنَّا قَبْلُ فِي أَهْلِنَا مُشْفِقِينَ
mots.tr: innā kunnā qablu fī ahlinā mushfiqīn
mots.racine: أ ه ل / ش ف ق
mots.sens: we were formerly fearful among our people

versets.ar: فَمَنَّ اللَّهُ عَلَيْنَا وَوَقَانَا عَذَابَ السَّمُومِ
versets.tr: fa-manna llāhu ʿalaynā wa-waqānā ʿadhāba s-samūmi
versets.trad: God has favoured us and spared us the punishment of the scorching wind.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dwells on this word: had they not said “God has favoured us,” they might have contemplated their own fear; but the Real ravished them far from the contemplation of their apprehension, making them contemplate His favour upon them, until they said: “God has favoured us and spared us the punishment of the burning wind.” The elect ascribes nothing to his own fear: he refers all to the grace (minna) of God.

mots.racine: م ن ن
mots.sens: God favoured us

mots.racine: و ق ي / س م م
mots.sens: He spared us the scorching punishment

versets.ar: إِنَّا كُنَّا مِن قَبْلُ نَدْعُوهُ ۚ إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْبَرُّ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: innā kunnā min qablu nadʿūhu innahu huwa l-barru r-raḥīmu
versets.trad: Formerly we used to call upon Him; He is the Beneficent, the Compassionate!”
versets.commentaire: “Formerly we used to call upon Him; He is the Beneficent (al-Barr), the Compassionate.” The elect acknowledges that his present felicity is the fruit of his past invocation — but also of God's gratuitous goodness. The two Names — al-Barr (who spreads good) and al-Raḥīm (the Merciful) — say that the gift always exceeds the merit: God gives by beneficence, not by debt.

mots.racine: د ع و
mots.sens: formerly we called upon Him

mots.racine: ب ر ر / ر ح م
mots.sens: He is the Beneficent, the Compassionate

versets.ar: فَذَكِّرْ فَمَا أَنتَ بِنِعْمَتِ رَبِّكَ بِكَاهِنٍ وَلَا مَجْنُونٍ
versets.tr: fa-dhakkir fa-mā anta bi-niʿmati rabbika bi-kāhinin wa-lā majnūnin
versets.trad: So remind! By the grace of your Lord, you are neither a soothsayer nor a madman!
versets.commentaire: “Remind! By the grace of your Lord, you are neither soothsayer nor madman.” Qushayrī: they know there is in you neither divination nor madness; if they say it, it is by disparagement — the fool loosens his tongue against whoever insults him with what he yet knows him innocent of. Slander does not touch the Prophet's truth, guarded by divine grace; it only reveals the baseness of the slanderer.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر
mots.sens: remind

mots.ar: فَمَا أَنتَ بِنِعْمَتِ رَبِّكَ بِكَاهِنٍ وَلَا مَجْنُونٍ
mots.tr: fa-mā anta bi-niʿmati rabbika bi-kāhinin wa-lā majnūn
mots.racine: ن ع م / ك ه ن / ج ن ن
mots.sens: by your Lord's grace you are neither soothsayer nor madman

versets.ar: أَمْ يَقُولُونَ شَاعِرٌ نَّتَرَبَّصُ بِهِ رَيْبَ الْمَنُونِ
versets.tr: am yaqūlūna shāʿirun natarabbaṣu bihi rayba l-manūni
versets.trad: Or do they say: “A poet! We await for him the calamities of fate”?
versets.commentaire: “Do they say: a poet, for whom we await the blows of fate?” Qushayrī: we await that the days strike him, for such a one does not endure, he will die as soothsayers and poets died before him; it is said they hoped he would die young, like his father. They bet on time to make the truth vanish — but it is they whom time will carry off.

mots.racine: ش ع ر
mots.sens: or do they say: a poet

mots.racine: ر ب ص / ر ي ب / م ن ن
mots.sens: we await for him the blows of fate
mots.def: tarabbaṣa: to await; rayb al-manūn: the calamities of death.

versets.ar: قُلْ تَرَبَّصُوا فَإِنِّي مَعَكُم مِّنَ الْمُتَرَبِّصِينَ
versets.tr: qul tarabbaṣū fa-innī maʿakum mina l-mutarabbiṣīna
versets.trad: Say: “Await! I too am with you among those who await!”
versets.commentaire: “Say: await! I await with you.” Qushayrī reports that those who hoped for the Prophet's death all died before him: none should hope for another's death; rarely is one whose trade this is not outstripped by death before he attains what he covets. The mocking wait turns around: whoever watches for the end of the truth seals his own end.

mots.racine: ر ب ص
mots.sens: say: await

mots.racine: ر ب ص
mots.sens: I am with you among those who await

versets.ar: أَمْ تَأْمُرُهُمْ أَحْلَامُهُم بِهَٰذَا أَمْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ طَاغُونَ
versets.tr: am taʾmuruhum aḥlāmuhum bi-hādhā am hum qawmun ṭāghūna
versets.trad: Do their minds command them to this, or are they a rebellious people?
versets.commentaire: “Do their reasons (aḥlām) dictate this to them, or are they a rebellious people?” Qushayrī notes the irony: the Quraysh prided themselves on being “people of reason and wisdom” — to ascribe to them here the aḥlām is sarcasm. The truth is elsewhere: it is excess (ṭughyān) that drives them, not intelligence. Reason, if it were straight, would lead to faith.

mots.racine: أ م ر / ح ل م
mots.sens: do their reasons command them this?

mots.racine: ط غ ي
mots.sens: or are they rebellious?

versets.ar: أَمْ يَقُولُونَ تَقَوَّلَهُ ۚ بَل لَّا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: am yaqūlūna taqawwalahu bal lā yuʾminūna
versets.trad: Or do they say: “He has invented it”? No, rather they do not believe.
versets.commentaire: “Do they say: he has forged it?” Qushayrī: if they claim that you say this discourse of your own. The next verse challenges them to produce its equal. The root of their accusation is not doubt but refusal: “no, they do not believe” — they invent pretexts because their heart has already decided not to yield.

mots.racine: ق و ل
mots.sens: do they say: he has forged it?

mots.racine: أ م ن
mots.sens: no, they do not believe

versets.ar: فَلْيَأْتُوا بِحَدِيثٍ مِّثْلِهِ إِن كَانُوا صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: fa-l-yaʾtū bi-ḥadīthin mithlihi in kānū ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: Let them produce a discourse like it, if they are truthful.
versets.commentaire: “Let them produce a discourse like it, if they are truthful.” Qushayrī: if they claim you say this of yourself, let them produce its equal, if they are truthful in what they charge you with. The challenge (taḥaddī) is unanswerable: the Qurʾān is inimitable. Their inability to take it up proves the divine origin of the Word they deny.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ح د ث / م ث ل
mots.sens: let them bring a discourse like it

mots.racine: ص د ق
mots.sens: if they are truthful

versets.ar: أَمْ خُلِقُوا مِنْ غَيْرِ شَيْءٍ أَمْ هُمُ الْخَالِقُونَ
versets.tr: am khuliqū min ghayri shayʾin am humu l-khāliqūna
versets.trad: Were they created from nothing, or are they the creators?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: never! The matter is not so: it is God the Creator and they the created. The double interrogation tightens the argument: either they were born without cause — absurd —, or they created themselves — impossible. Only one truth remains: they have a Creator, to whom they owe all. To deny this is to deny the evidence of one's own origin.

mots.racine: خ ل ق
mots.sens: were they created from nothing?

mots.racine: خ ل ق
mots.sens: or are they the creators?

versets.ar: أَمْ خَلَقُوا السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ ۚ بَل لَّا يُوقِنُونَ
versets.tr: am khalaqū s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa bal lā yūqinūna
versets.trad: Did they create the heavens and the earth? No, rather they have no certainty!
versets.commentaire: “Did they create the heavens and the earth?” The absurdity bursts forth: whoever did not create himself did not create the universe. The conclusion falls: “no, they have no certainty (lā yūqinūn).” Their denial comes not from knowledge but from a lack of certainty: they refuse the evidence for want of yaqīn.

mots.racine: خ ل ق
mots.sens: did they create the heavens and earth?

mots.racine: ي ق ن
mots.sens: no, they have no certainty

versets.ar: أَمْ عِندَهُمْ خَزَائِنُ رَبِّكَ أَمْ هُمُ الْمُصَيْطِرُونَ
versets.tr: am ʿindahum khazāʾinu rabbika am humu l-muṣayṭirūna
versets.trad: Do they possess the treasures of your Lord, or are they in charge?
versets.commentaire: “Do they hold your Lord's treasures?” — the stores of His provision and power. “Or are they the controlling masters (muṣayṭirūn)?” The interrogation dismantles their pretension: they possess neither the divine treasures nor power over men. Possessing nothing, by what right do they contest the One who possesses all?

mots.racine: خ ز ن / ر ب ب
mots.sens: do they hold your Lord's treasures?

mots.racine: س ي ط ر
mots.sens: or are they the controllers?

versets.ar: أَمْ لَهُمْ سُلَّمٌ يَسْتَمِعُونَ فِيهِ ۖ فَلْيَأْتِ مُسْتَمِعُهُم بِسُلْطَانٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: am lahum sullamun yastamiʿūna fīhi fa-l-yaʾti mustamiʿuhum bi-sulṭānin mubīnin
versets.trad: Do they have a ladder by which they listen? Then let their listener bring a clear authority!
versets.commentaire: “Do they have a ladder (sullam) by which they climb to listen” to what is woven in the heavens? Then let their listener produce a clear proof (sulṭān mubīn). The irony is biting: they have no access to the heavenly world to ground their denials. Their opposition to the Qurʾān rests on no authority, on no real listening to the unseen.

mots.racine: س ل م / س م ع
mots.sens: do they have a ladder by which they listen?

mots.racine: س م ع / س ل ط / ب ي ن
mots.sens: let their listener bring a clear proof

versets.ar: أَمْ لَهُ الْبَنَاتُ وَلَكُمُ الْبَنُونَ
versets.tr: am lahu l-banātu wa-lakumu l-banūna
versets.trad: Does He have daughters while you have sons?
versets.commentaire: “To Him daughters and to you sons?” The pagans ascribed daughters (the angels) to God while scorning the birth of daughters. The absurdity of their theology bursts forth: they lend to God what they disdain for themselves. The partition they imagine is unjust — and reveals the incoherence of whoever invents gods to suit himself.

mots.racine: ب ن ي
mots.sens: to Him daughters and to you sons?

versets.ar: أَمْ تَسْأَلُهُمْ أَجْرًا فَهُم مِّن مَّغْرَمٍ مُّثْقَلُونَ
versets.tr: am tasʾaluhum ajran fa-hum min maghramin muthqalūna
versets.trad: Do you ask them a wage, so they are weighed down by debt?
versets.commentaire: “Do you ask them a wage, that would burden them with debt?” Qushayrī: do you claim, for transmitting the message, a reward that would charge them with a loss, to the point of putting them off following you? The Prophet asks for nothing: his message is free. No material pretext can therefore justify their refusal.

mots.racine: س أ ل / أ ج ر
mots.sens: do you ask them a wage?

mots.racine: غ ر م / ث ق ل
mots.sens: so they are weighed down by debt
mots.def: maghram: debt, burden; muthqal: weighed down.

versets.ar: أَمْ عِندَهُمُ الْغَيْبُ فَهُمْ يَكْتُبُونَ
versets.tr: am ʿindahumu l-ghaybu fa-hum yaktubūna
versets.trad: Do they possess the Unseen, so they write it down?
versets.commentaire: “Do they hold the Unseen (ghayb), that they write it?” The unseen is known to God alone: no man has access to it to record its content. Their pretension to judge the Qurʾān and the Hereafter rests on nothing. The ghayb escapes all human grasp; God alone knows it and reveals it to whom He wills.

mots.racine: غ ي ب / ك ت ب
mots.sens: do they hold the Unseen, that they write it?

versets.ar: أَمْ يُرِيدُونَ كَيْدًا ۖ فَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا هُمُ الْمَكِيدُونَ
versets.tr: am yurīdūna kaydan fa-lladhīna kafarū humu l-makīdūna
versets.trad: Do they intend a plot? But the disbelievers are the ones outwitted!
versets.commentaire: “Do they intend a plot?” — a scheme against the Prophet and the believers. But “the disbelievers are the ones outwitted (makīdūn).” The plot recoils upon its author: whoever schemes against the truth falls into his own trap. The divine decree foils every stratagem; the human intrigue snares itself in what it thought to set.

mots.racine: ر و د / ك ي د
mots.sens: do they intend a plot?

mots.racine: ك ف ر / ك ي د
mots.sens: the disbelievers are the outwitted

versets.ar: أَمْ لَهُمْ إِلَٰهٌ غَيْرُ اللَّهِ ۚ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: am lahum ilāhun ghayru llāhi subḥāna llāhi ʿammā yushrikūna
versets.trad: Do they have a god other than God? Glory be to God above what they associate!
versets.commentaire: “Do they have a god other than God” who would do anything of what God does? Qushayrī: transcendence to Him above that! The litany of “am” (or…) culminates here: after dismantling all their pretensions, the verse proclaims tawḥīd. No other god acts; He alone creates, provides, decrees. The tasbīḥ (glory to God) closes the argument on pure transcendence.

mots.racine: أ ل ه
mots.sens: do they have a god other than God?

mots.racine: س ب ح / ش ر ك
mots.sens: glory to God above what they associate

versets.ar: وَإِن يَرَوْا كِسْفًا مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ سَاقِطًا يَقُولُوا سَحَابٌ مَّرْكُومٌ
versets.tr: wa-in yaraw kisfan mina s-samāʾi sāqiṭan yaqūlū saḥābun markūmun
versets.trad: If they saw a fragment of the sky falling, they would say: “Heaped-up clouds.”
versets.commentaire: “If they saw a fragment of the sky falling, they would say: heaped-up clouds.” Qushayrī: whatever signs they see, they do not believe; even if We opened them a gate of the sky and they saw with their eyes, they would say: our sights have been bewitched, this is neither vision nor evidence. Obstinate blindness reinterprets every sign so as not to yield.

mots.ar: وَإِن يَرَوْا كِسْفًا مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ سَاقِطًا
mots.tr: wa-in yaraw kisfan mina s-samāʾi sāqiṭan
mots.racine: ر أ ي / ك س ف / س ق ط
mots.sens: if they saw a fragment of the sky falling

mots.racine: س ح ب / ر ك م
mots.sens: they would say: heaped clouds

versets.ar: فَذَرْهُمْ حَتَّىٰ يُلَاقُوا يَوْمَهُمُ الَّذِي فِيهِ يُصْعَقُونَ
versets.tr: fa-dharhum ḥattā yulāqū yawmahumu lladhī fīhi yuṣʿaqūna
versets.trad: So leave them until they meet their Day, on which they will be thunderstruck;
versets.commentaire: “Leave them until they meet their Day on which they will be thunderstruck (yuṣʿaqūn).” Qushayrī: turn from them until the Day they will die. The Prophet has transmitted; the obstinate refusal no longer falls to him. The Day of the thunderbolt — death or the Resurrection — will settle what the warning could not bend.

mots.racine: و ذ ر / ل ق ي
mots.sens: leave them until they meet their Day
mots.def: wadhara: to leave; lāqā: to meet.

mots.racine: ص ع ق
mots.sens: on which they are thunderstruck

versets.ar: يَوْمَ لَا يُغْنِي عَنْهُمْ كَيْدُهُمْ شَيْئًا وَلَا هُمْ يُنصَرُونَ
versets.tr: yawma lā yughnī ʿanhum kayduhum shayʾan wa-lā hum yunṣarūna
versets.trad: the Day their plotting will avail them nothing, nor will they be helped.
versets.commentaire: “The Day their plotting will avail them nothing, nor will they be helped.” The scheming of the earlier verses (verse 42) collapses: on the decisive Day, the stratagem is powerless and help absent. No refuge outside God — the constant lesson: whoever bet on cunning against the truth finds himself without defence or ally.

mots.ar: يَوْمَ لَا يُغْنِي عَنْهُمْ كَيْدُهُمْ شَيْئًا
mots.tr: yawma lā yughnī ʿanhum kayduhum shayʾan
mots.racine: غ ن ي / ك ي د
mots.sens: the day their plot avails nothing

mots.racine: ن ص ر
mots.sens: nor will they be helped

versets.ar: وَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا عَذَابًا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-inna lilladhīna ẓalamū ʿadhāban dūna dhālika wa-lākinna aktharahum lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: The wrongdoers will surely have a punishment before that, but most of them do not know.
versets.commentaire: “The wrongdoers have a punishment short of that one.” Qushayrī: before the Day of Resurrection, they suffer the punishment of killing and captivity, the humiliation and disgrace of Badr and elsewhere; but most do not know that God makes His religion triumph. The punishment begins in this world, a forerunner of the Day; but the blind one does not recognize the hand of God in it.

mots.ar: وَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا عَذَابًا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ
mots.tr: wa-inna lilladhīna ẓalamū ʿadhāban dūna dhālik
mots.racine: ظ ل م / ع ذ ب
mots.sens: the wrongdoers have a punishment short of that
mots.def: ẓalama: to wrong; dūn: short of.

mots.racine: ك ث ر / ع ل م
mots.sens: but most do not know

versets.ar: وَاصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ فَإِنَّكَ بِأَعْيُنِنَا ۖ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ حِينَ تَقُومُ
versets.tr: wa-ṣbir li-ḥukmi rabbika fa-innaka bi-aʿyuninā wa-sabbiḥ bi-ḥamdi rabbika ḥīna taqūmu
versets.trad: So bear patiently the judgment of your Lord, for you are before Our eyes. And glorify the praise of your Lord when you rise.
versets.commentaire: “Be patient for the judgment of your Lord, for you are before Our eyes (bi-aʿyuninā).” Qushayrī: you are in Our sight, in Our protection; in this, an easing for him who endures patience. The plural “Our eyes” speaks the surplus of solicitude. And he concludes: patience at God's decree is heavy, but when one knows the Lord's gaze set upon oneself, it becomes easy and light.

mots.racine: ص ب ر / ح ك م / ع ي ن
mots.sens: be patient for your Lord's judgment, you are before Our eyes
mots.def: ḥukm: judgment; bi-aʿyuninā: before Our gaze.

mots.racine: س ب ح / ح م د / ق و م
mots.sens: glorify your Lord when you rise

versets.ar: وَمِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَسَبِّحْهُ وَإِدْبَارَ النُّجُومِ
versets.tr: wa-mina l-layli fa-sabbiḥhu wa-idbāra n-nujūmi
versets.trad: And glorify Him in part of the night and at the fading of the stars!
versets.commentaire: “Glorify Him in part of the night and at the fading of the stars.” Qushayrī: in this verse, an indication that He commands him to remember Him at every time, that no moment be empty of His remembrance. And he closes the sūra: patience at God's decree is hard; but whoever knows that the Lord sees him finds it easy and light. The continuous tasbīḥ is the easing of patience: the heart that glorifies ceaselessly remains under the gaze that upholds it.

mots.racine: ل ي ل / س ب ح
mots.sens: part of the night, glorify Him

mots.racine: د ب ر / ن ج م
mots.sens: and at the fading of the stars
